title: Validate legacy import SQL
description: Review the legacy practice-pal import generator and update the book, section, exercise, artist, and song SQL so it matches the current Supabase schema. Do not run the import script.
close_reason: Abandoned one-shot legacy import SQL attempt; generated files were removed and no import work is being kept in this branch.

title: Add Perles tree view for top-level roadmap
description: Add a Perles Tree view rooted at the top-level Get Practice Pal Roadmap epic and organize existing work under that epic.
close_reason: Added the Perles Tree view and exported the updated one-root Beads hierarchy.

title: Get Practice Pal Roadmap
description: Top-level epic for the migrated Get Practice Pal work tree.

title: Port beans issues to Beads
description: Migrate the existing .beans tracker contents into the initialized Beads database while preserving issue IDs, status, type, parent hierarchy, and useful historical context.
close_reason: Migrated 80 legacy beans into Beads, exported .beads/issues.jsonl, and removed the old .beans tracker files.

title: Frontend: show linked book covers and metadata
description:
Display linked external book metadata in the library experience once a user book is connected to an ISBN/provider record.

## Scope
- Show linked cover art where book summaries and detail pages need it
- Surface canonical title, authors, and release year when available
- Keep user-entered title/notes usable when external metadata is missing or intentionally skipped
- Link users back into the picker/edit flow to change or remove the external book association

title: Frontend: handle no book search results
description:
Handle cases where external book lookup returns no usable results.

## Scope
- Show a clear no-results state for ISBN and text searches
- Let users continue with manual book entry
- Offer retry or edit-search affordances where appropriate
- Avoid blocking book creation when lookup fails or finds nothing

title: Frontend: add book search picker and confirmation UI
description:
Add a UI for reviewing external book search results and confirming which result should be linked to a user book.

## Scope
- Show candidate covers, titles, authors, release years, and ISBNs
- Let users choose a result or skip linking
- Preserve user-entered book fields when linking external metadata
- Make the confirmation flow work for both create and edit paths

title: Frontend: add book metadata search triggers
description:
Add UI entry points that trigger external book metadata lookup from the book workflow.

## Scope
- Trigger search from ISBN entry when available
- Trigger search from title/author fields when ISBN is absent
- Make lookup actions discoverable without interrupting existing create/edit flows
- Handle loading and retry states for search requests
close_reason: Implemented frontend metadata search triggers in the book create/edit flows. Added a client search panel with ISBN and title/author inputs, loading state, retry-by-search behavior, basic result previews, and no-result feedback without interrupting normal book saves. Verified with npm run test:google-books, npm run lint, npm run typecheck, and npm run build.

title: Backend: normalize Google Books responses
description:
Normalize Google Books API responses into the app external-book shape before persistence or display.

## Scope
- Extract ISBN-10, ISBN-13, title, authors, published year, cover URLs, and provider identifiers
- Handle missing or partial fields without breaking the UI
- Prefer stable high-quality cover URLs when multiple image sizes exist
- Add focused tests for representative Google Books payloads
close_reason: Extracted Google Books response normalization into a testable normalizeGoogleBooksVolume function. It extracts provider IDs, ISBN-10/13, title/subtitle, authors, published year/date, page count, language, canonical URL, and HTTPS cover URLs while tolerating partial payloads. Added focused Node test coverage for representative full, partial, and invalid payloads. Verified with npm run test:google-books, npm run lint, and npm run typecheck.

title: Backend: add Google Books lookup
description:
Add a backend lookup path that searches Google Books for candidate book metadata from ISBNs, titles, and authors.

## Scope
- Add a server-side Google Books API client
- Support lookup by ISBN and search by title/author text
- Keep API credentials and request limits server-side
- Return typed candidate results to the library UI
close_reason: Implemented a server-side Google Books lookup path. Added GOOGLE_BOOKS_API_KEY to .env.example, a typed Google Books client with ISBN and title/author search support, HTTPS cover URL normalization, and a library server action that returns typed candidates to future UI flows. Verified with npm run lint, npm run typecheck, keyed title lookup, and keyed ISBN lookup using a known ISBN. One ISBN-specific request returned Google 503, but alternate keyed ISBN lookup succeeded.

title: DB: link user books to external books
description:
Update the current books model so app books remain user-owned records and can optionally point at canonical external book metadata.

## Scope
- Add an optional foreign key from user books to external books
- Keep user-specific fields editable and scoped to the owning user
- Preserve existing book records during migration
- Update backend queries/types to include linked external metadata when present
close_reason: Implemented and applied the user-book to external-book link. Added a nullable books.external_book_id foreign key to external_books with an index, updated library snapshot queries to include linked external metadata, and preserved externalBookId through create/update book actions and edit forms. Verified with npm run lint, npm run typecheck, psql migration application, and post-migration column/index queries.

title: DB: add external books table
description:
Add a database table for canonical external book metadata linked from providers such as Google Books.

## Scope
- Store provider identifiers and ISBNs
- Store canonical title, authors, release year, cover image URLs, and source metadata needed by the app
- Define uniqueness rules so repeated lookups reuse existing external book rows
- Add the required Supabase migration and data access shape
close_reason: Implemented and applied the external_books database foundation. Added a Supabase migration with provider/provider-book uniqueness, ISBN indexes, cover/metadata fields, timestamps, RLS policies, and the updated_at trigger. Added typed ExternalBook/ExternalBookUpsert shapes plus upsertExternalBook and findExternalBooksByIsbn data helpers. Verified with npm run lint, npm run typecheck, psql connectivity, migration application, and post-migration table/policy queries.

title: Link books to ISBNs
description:
Add an ISBN/external-book linking workflow so Practice Pal books can be connected to canonical book metadata and covers.

## Goals
- Show and link book covers from external metadata
- Surface canonical titles and authors alongside user book records
- Include release year when available
- Keep user-owned book data separate from reusable external book metadata

## Scope
- Database support for external books and user-book linkage
- Backend lookup and normalization for Google Books responses
- Frontend search, confirmation, picker, and empty-result flows

title: Fix sessions started from setlists not loading setlist items
description:
Starting a session from a setlist should preload the setlist items into the active session, but the current flow does not appear to do that reliably.

## Todo
- [x] Trace the session start flow for setlist-backed sessions
- [x] Fix preloading so setlist items appear in the new active session
- [x] Verify the behavior in the browser before finalizing

## Summary of Changes

Fixed session startup from setlists so setlist items are copied into the new active session with seeded tempos and correct ordering.

title: Add confirm dialog for exercise and destructive deletes
description:
Add confirm dialogs for destructive delete actions like deleting exercises so accidental clicks are less risky.

## Todo
- [ ] Add a reusable confirm dialog pattern for destructive actions
- [ ] Use it for exercise deletes
- [ ] Extend it to similar destructive flows later

title: Remove book-level goal tempo from backend
description:
Book-level goal tempo adds noise to the library flow and should be removed from the data model and backend APIs in a later pass.

## Scope
- Remove book default goal tempo from schema and services
- Update frontend forms and stats assumptions accordingly
- Plan migration for existing data

## Summary of Changes

Removed book-level default goal tempo from the backend code path, added a migration to drop `books.default_goal_tempo`, and verified only section-level tempo handling remains in the active app code. The migration has been applied in Supabase.

title: Split exercise detail into dedicated route
description:
Move exercise-specific editing and progress off the section page into its own route so section pages stay scannable.

## Todo
- [ ] Add /library/books/[bookId]/sections/[sectionId]/exercises/[exerciseId] route
- [ ] Make section exercise rows link to the dedicated exercise page
- [ ] Simplify the section page after moving the exercise-specific detail out

title: Remove position fields from the visible UI
description:
Position is currently exposed as a raw field in many flows. Remove those visible position controls from the UI so ordering does not leak implementation details into normal workflows.

## Todo
- [x] Audit visible position inputs and labels across sessions, library, setlists, and related forms
- [x] Remove or hide position from the default UI flow
- [x] Verify ordering still feels understandable without explicit position controls

## Summary of Changes

Removed visible position and display-order controls from the default UI across sessions, setlists, and library flows while preserving backend ordering through hidden fields. Session item editing now focuses on tempo, setlist items no longer show or request positions, and library section/exercise screens no longer expose raw ordering values. Browser verification confirmed the flows felt cleaner.

title: Implement session logging and editing backend actions
description:
Support the session lifecycle and practiced-item logging on the backend.

## Scope
- Start, pause, resume, and end session actions
- Session item association and tempo capture
- Session note editing and tempo updates
- Validation for tempo and ownership

## Summary of Changes

Implemented backend session services for current session lookup, recent history, start/pause/resume/end operations, note editing, and practiced-item tempo upserts with validation.

title: Enforce minimum form-to-button spacing
description:
Make sure forms consistently leave enough vertical space between the last field and the primary action button, especially in add/edit modals.

## Todo
- [x] Audit cramped form layouts such as add book and add artist
- [x] Apply a shared minimum spacing rule between fields and action buttons
- [x] Verify the forms feel more breathable without growing unnecessarily

## Summary of Changes\n\nStandardized the minimum spacing between modal form fields and footer actions, removed the extra inner-card layer from create modals, and aligned Close and submit actions into a single footer row.

title: Add view session page
description:
Add a dedicated page for viewing past sessions in detail instead of limiting session history to summary rows.

## Todo
- [x] Add a route for viewing an individual past session
- [x] Show logged items, notes, timing, and source setlist context when present
- [x] Link recent session rows into the detail page

## Summary of Changes\n\nAdded the /sessions/[sessionId] route, linked recent sessions into the detail view, and built a dedicated past-session layout that surfaces timing, notes, logged items, and source setlist context more clearly than the list row view.

title: Make section and similar subpage cards fully clickable
description:
Navigation into deeper detail pages should be as easy as the library book cards. Extend whole-card click targets to sections and similar drill-in rows where it improves flow.

## Todo
- [ ] Identify subpage entry rows that still rely on small text buttons
- [ ] Expand those rows into larger click targets while preserving secondary actions
- [x] Verify navigation feels faster on desktop and mobile

## Summary of Changes

Updated the book section list so section cards themselves are the drill-in target instead of relying on small inline buttons. Removed the preview/details affordance and extra metadata from those rows, keeping only the section title and exercise count. Browser verification confirmed this flow felt right.

title: Library Embedded Progress
description:
Move progress and completion stats out of the standalone Stats page and into the Library detail hierarchy so progress is visible where users manage books, sections, exercises, artists, and songs.

## Success Criteria
- Book, section, exercise, artist, and song views surface relevant progress inline
- The standalone /stats page is no longer the primary stats surface
- Library pages remain readable while exposing useful practice progress

title: Add reusable nested practice item picker
description:
Build a reusable nested selector component that later session and setlist flows can use to choose exercises and songs.

## Scope
- Nested rendering for books -> sections -> exercises and artists -> songs
- Search/filter support
- Selection affordances and summary
- Initial integration on the library page

## Summary of Changes

Built a reusable nested practice item picker with hierarchical library rendering, search support, and selection summary. Integrated the picker into the library flow and reused the same library item mapping approach for sessions and setlists.

title: Make sidebar outline black with dropshadow
description:
Refine the sidebar chrome so the rail outline/shadow treatment feels intentional without changing the accepted inner card styling.

## Todo
- [x] Update sidebar outer chrome treatment
- [x] Preserve the accepted inner nav card styling
- [x] Verify the sidebar feels stronger without becoming visually heavy

## Summary of Changes\n\nAdjusted the sidebar chrome to match the accepted card language, restored the inner nav styling, improved default icon contrast, and kept nested route highlighting stable while the side rail remained visually lighter than the failed black-fill pass.

title: Implement Backend MVP milestone
description:
Execute the Backend MVP milestone by introducing user-scoped auth, the core domain schema, session logging, and stats query support.

## Todo
- [x] Break Backend MVP epics into executable child beans
- [x] Implement user auth and row-level ownership
- [x] Define and migrate the core schema
- [x] Add session logging and post-session editing backend paths
- [x] Add stats query support for tempo progress and completion
- [x] Summarize backend decisions and open follow-ups

## Summary of Changes

Implemented the Backend MVP code layer around Supabase magic-link auth, protected shell routing, the core SQL schema and RLS policies, and server-side services for library, setlists, sessions, and stats queries.

## Open Follow-ups

- Apply the migration to a live Supabase project
- Add the Supabase env vars in local and Vercel environments
- Exercise the auth flow and data services against a real database before closing the milestone


Validated against a live Supabase project: the SQL migration applied successfully, magic-link sign-in worked, and sign-out returned the user to the login screen.

title: Standardize on DaisyUI components and theme primitives
description:
The UI still uses too many one-off button, field, and surface treatments instead of leaning on DaisyUI consistently.

## Todo
- [x] Audit custom controls and component variants that duplicate DaisyUI primitives
- [x] Consolidate buttons, fields, cards, and typography onto a smaller shared component/theme set
- [x] Verify the app feels more consistent without losing the accepted visual direction

## Summary of Changes

Standardized the main workflow screens around a smaller DaisyUI-style primitive set by introducing shared outline and field treatments, expanding the shared submit button variants, and replacing most one-off ghost-plus-border and input-bordered combinations across library, setlists, sessions, stats, login, and modal surfaces. Also fixed primary and error button contrast so high-emphasis actions like Add section and End session render with white text.

title: Simplify active session layout and note save flow
description:
Tighten the active session page so the order and actions match the actual workflow.

## Todo
- [x] Reorder active session sections to logged items, add item, then notes/end session
- [x] Remove the standalone notes save action and save notes when ending the session
- [x] Verify the new flow in the browser

## Summary of Changes

Simplified the active session workflow to match the actual practice flow: logged items first, add item second, and notes paired directly with ending the session.

title: Remove long page title copy
description:
Trim the long descriptive sentence under page titles across the app where it is only restating the page purpose.

## Todo
- [x] Audit page-level subtitle copy on library, sessions, setlists, stats, and related screens
- [x] Remove or shorten low-value page subtitle text
- [x] Verify the pages still orient users without the extra sentence copy

## Summary of Changes\n\nRemoved low-value page subtitle copy across the top-level workflow pages, switched the top-level views to eyebrow-led headers, and tuned eyebrow sizing so the pages stay oriented without long repeated sentences.

title: Refine dense forms and list screens
description:
Improve the most information-dense web screens so forms, item lists, and summaries are easier to scan and act on.

## Todo
- [x] Tighten hierarchy and spacing on the Library management screen
- [x] Improve Setlists form and item-list readability
- [x] Improve Sessions history and active-item readability where it still feels crowded
- [x] Verify lint, typecheck, and build
- [x] Summarize the refinement pass and close the task

## Summary of Changes

Completed the dense-screen refinement pass across library, setlists, and sessions, then verified the result in the browser and with project checks. This task now represents the accepted first-pass cleanup before the later drag-and-drop and follow-on polish work.

title: Add magic-link auth flow and protected app shell
description:
Implement the MVP authentication flow with Supabase magic links and protect user-scoped areas.

## Scope
- Login page and magic-link request flow
- Auth confirmation route and session persistence
- Protected app shell and logout support
- Auth-aware session retrieval for server code

## Summary of Changes

Added a Supabase magic-link login page, auth confirmation and sign-out routes, protected the main app shell when Supabase is configured, and added auth-aware session helpers plus proxy-based session refresh.

title: Document no-direct-commit master rule
description:
Add an explicit AGENTS.md workflow rule that changes must not be committed directly to master and must go through the normal branch + PR + merge flow.

## Todo
- [x] Update AGENTS.md with the no-direct-to-master rule
- [x] Verify the instruction is clear and committed in project guidance

## Summary of Changes

- added an explicit AGENTS.md workflow rule forbidding direct commits to `master`
- clarified that all changes must continue to go through the normal branch + PR + merge flow

title: Embed progress on book, section, and exercise views
description:
Expose completion and tempo progress directly inside the book hierarchy.

## Todo
- [ ] Add book-level completion to /library/books/[bookId]
- [ ] Add section-level progress to /library/books/[bookId]/sections/[sectionId]
- [ ] Surface per-exercise progress inline in the section view

title: Redesign setlists flow away from nested all-in-one screen
description:
Setlists currently feel too noisy and nested. Redesign the setlists workflow to mirror the calmer library model: lighter overview, focused detail/edit screens, and less stacked inline structure.

## Todo
- [x] Define a clearer setlists information architecture for web
- [x] Replace dense inline editing with focused setlist flows
- [x] Verify the updated setlists UI feels lighter on desktop and mobile

## Summary of Changes

Reworked setlists to mirror the calmer library flow. Added a lighter overview page, focused setlist detail pages, and reduced inline nesting in the editing flow.

title: Beautification V2
description:
Second-pass UI polish focused on simplifying the remaining dense or redundant surfaces after the first beautification milestone.

## Success Criteria
- Remaining workflow screens are flatter and more direct
- Auth and library detail screens have less redundant chrome
- The UI feels cleaner without expanding product scope

## Summary of Changes

- simplified the remaining redundant book-detail chrome by moving editing into the hero area with a read-first Edit toggle
- reduced the login page to a lighter single-card layout with clearer primary action emphasis
- kept the accepted product scope and visual direction while making both surfaces flatter and easier to parse

title: Reduce nested card stacks across workflow screens
description:
Several pages still stack cards inside cards, creating unnecessary visual layers. Simplify those layouts so sections read as flatter, more direct surfaces.

## Todo
- [ ] Identify remaining card-in-card patterns on sessions, library, setlists, and stats
- [ ] Flatten or remove redundant parent surfaces where they do not add structure
- [x] Verify the result feels lighter without losing grouping clarity

## Summary of Changes

Removed several unnecessary card-within-card layers across sessions, stats, setlists, and section detail screens. Active sessions now use a flatter section stack without an enclosing active-session card, stats no longer nests progress points inside an extra panel or empty side column, and item/exercise/session-history rows now use simpler row surfaces instead of mini cards. Browser validation confirmed the result feels lighter.

title: Reduce remaining custom CSS and class sprawl
description:
Keep the accepted punchier visual direction, but continue reducing custom CSS and repeated per-element class stacks by leaning more on shared components and standard theme primitives.

## Todo
- [x] Audit the remaining custom surface and typography classes in globals.css and identify what can collapse into shared component primitives
- [x] Replace repeated class stacks across workflow screens with a smaller shared component/layout base
- [x] Verify the app keeps the accepted visual style while relying less on custom per-element styling

## Summary of Changes

- added a shared UI primitive layer for page heroes, panels, stats, empty states, fields, inputs, textareas, card links, and drag handles
- moved the main library, sessions, setlists, stats, login, modal, select, section builder, and draggable row flows onto the shared base instead of repeated one-off class stacks
- removed dead custom field/surface CSS from globals.css and kept the accepted visual direction with lighter shared markup
- preserved the existing sidebar look while fixing nested route highlighting for library and setlist subpages, and slightly strengthened list-row borders for dense data rows

title: Study practicepal UI reference
description:
Review the existing PracticePal React Native app in ~/devel/practicepal to capture the visual system, colors, highlight treatments, spacing, and general IO patterns that should inform the web app beautification pass.

## Todo
- [x] Locate the main theme, shared UI primitives, and representative screens in the React Native app
- [x] Document the reusable visual patterns to preserve in the web app
- [x] Identify any interaction or layout ideas that need translation for the web shell
- [x] Summarize findings and close the task

## Notes

Reference the React Native app for palette, typography hierarchy, highlight treatments, and input affordances only. Do not copy screen layouts directly; adapt them for responsive web flows with the current side-rail shell and phone-first breakpoints.

## Summary of Changes

Reviewed the practice-pal React Native app and extracted only the reusable visual guidance for the web app: red/slate/orange palette, calmer white and slate surfaces, bold heading hierarchy, and stronger but simple input affordances. Explicitly translated the reference away from mobile app layouts and toward the existing responsive side-rail web shell.

title: Improve UI copy and microcopy across workflow screens
description:
Refine the text throughout the app now that the layout direction is settling. Focus on clearer labels, calmer supporting copy, and less noisy instructional text without changing the accepted page structure.

## Todo
- [x] Audit noisy or redundant text across Library, Setlists, Sessions, and Stats
- [x] Rewrite headings, descriptions, button labels, and helper text for clarity and brevity
- [x] Verify the revised copy still fits responsive layouts cleanly

## Summary of Changes

Trimmed copy across the shell and core workflow screens so the UI relies more on titles, inputs, and structure instead of explanatory paragraphs. Kept only the small amount of text that still helps with decisions or empty states.

title: Define Supabase schema and RLS policies for MVP tables
description:
Create the SQL schema for library, setlists, sessions, and practiced items with user ownership and row-level security.

## Scope
- Core tables and foreign keys
- Ownership columns and RLS policies
- Goal tempo and hierarchy support
- Database-side integrity constraints

## Summary of Changes

Added the Backend MVP SQL migration covering profiles, library, setlists, sessions, practiced items, ownership columns, indexes, triggers, and row-level security policies.

title: Trim redundant modal copy
description:
Reduce repetitive labels and repeated title/action wording inside creation modals, especially library flows like Add artist.

## Todo
- [x] Audit repeated modal title/label/button wording
- [x] Tighten labels and action copy so the modal does not repeat itself
- [x] Verify modals still feel clear with less text

## Summary of Changes\n\nTrimmed repeated modal headings, labels, and button wording across the create flows, especially in Library and Setlists, while keeping the forms understandable after the copy reduction.

title: Implement backend services for library and setlist data
description:
Add server-side data access and write boundaries for the core library and setlist models.

## Scope
- Typed server-side query helpers
- Create and update flows for library entities
- Setlist create and edit support
- User-scoped reads and writes

## Summary of Changes

Implemented server-side library and setlist services for user-scoped reads and writes, including creation flows and setlist item replacement support.

title: Add exercise delete action on exercise detail page
description:
Add a safe delete path for exercises on the dedicated exercise detail page instead of the section overview.

## Todo
- [ ] Add backend deleteExercise support
- [ ] Add server action for deleting from the exercise detail page
- [ ] Surface a delete control on /library/books/[bookId]/sections/[sectionId]/exercises/[exerciseId]

title: Deploy and validate Initial Structure milestone
description:
Keep the Initial Structure milestone open until the app is deployed and manually confirmed working.

## Todo
- [x] Simplify the shell to the single side-rail pattern
- [x] Push the branch and connect the repo to Vercel
- [x] Confirm preview or production deployment succeeds
- [x] Manually verify the host pages load correctly in deployment
- [x] Close the milestone after user confirmation

## Summary of Changes

Confirmed the Vercel preview deployment worked and the deployed host pages loaded correctly. The Initial Structure milestone is now verified in deployment, not just locally.

title: Implement library management frontend
description:
Implement the Library management frontend on top of the Backend MVP services.

## Todo
- [x] Break the library epic into executable frontend feature work
- [x] Replace the placeholder library page with real data-driven UI
- [x] Add CRUD forms for books, sections, exercises, artists, and songs
- [x] Add goal tempo inputs and inherited default presentation
- [x] Add nested selectors usable by later session and setlist flows
- [x] Verify library flows locally and summarize follow-ups

## Summary of Changes

Implemented the live library management experience with verified create and update flows for both hierarchy types, goal tempo/default presentation, and a reusable nested selector feeding later session and setlist work. Browser verification confirmed the full UI flow now works end to end.

title: Add library CRUD actions and forms
description:
Replace the placeholder library page with live create and update flows for books, sections, exercises, artists, and songs.

## Scope
- Server actions for create and update flows
- Data-driven library management page
- Goal tempo inputs and inherited defaults
- Book and artist hierarchy editing surfaces

## Summary of Changes

Added server actions and live create and update forms for books, sections, exercises, artists, and songs. The library page now uses real Supabase-backed data and exposes goal tempo inputs and inherited defaults.

title: Publish Backend MVP changes
description:
Publish the completed Backend MVP implementation by creating a branch, committing the backend/auth/schema changes, pushing to GitHub, and opening a draft PR.

## Todo
- [x] Create a publish branch from master
- [x] Stage the Backend MVP code and bean changes
- [x] Commit and push the branch
- [x] Open a draft PR with deployment notes
- [x] Call out required Vercel env vars for deployment

## Summary of Changes

Backend MVP was published, validated, and merged earlier in PR #2. This tracker bean was left in-progress, but the publish branch, PR, and deployment notes all happened successfully.

title: UX Improvements
description:
Broader user-experience improvements that go beyond the current MVP polish pass, including onboarding flexibility and a real marketing/about surface.

## Success Criteria
- Auth flow supports more ways to get into the app
- Visitors can understand and try the product more easily
- Public landing/about experience explains the core value clearly

title: Public home/about page with screenshots
description:
Create a public landing/about page that explains the product and shows the core features with screenshots.

## Todo
- [ ] Define the key marketing sections and screenshot story
- [ ] Build the public page layout and content
- [ ] Verify it explains the app clearly on desktop and mobile

title: Add stats query helpers for progress and completion
description:
Implement reusable backend queries for the MVP stats surfaces.

## Scope
- Item tempo history and global max queries
- Progress-to-goal calculations
- Book and section completion calculations
- Time-window filtering for common reporting ranges

## Summary of Changes

Added stats query helpers for item tempo history, progress-to-goal calculations, and book completion calculations across common reporting windows.

title: Add dev-only password auth fallback
description:
Add a temporary dev-only password auth fallback behind an environment flag so frontend testing can continue when Supabase magic-link email limits are hit.

## Todo
- [x] Add password sign-in server action
- [x] Gate the fallback UI behind a dev-only env flag
- [x] Update docs and env examples
- [x] Verify lint, typecheck, and build

## Summary of Changes

Added password sign-in and sign-up server actions behind ENABLE_DEV_PASSWORD_LOGIN=true, exposed the fallback UI on /login only when that flag is enabled, updated .env.example and README.md, and verified npm run lint, npm run typecheck, and npm run build.

## Summary of Changes

title: Expand Supabase auth options
description:
Add more sign-in and sign-up options supported by Supabase so onboarding is more flexible.

## Todo
- [ ] Decide which additional auth providers or flows to support first
- [ ] Implement the provider configuration and UI
- [ ] Verify the added auth options work in local and deployed environments

title: Simplify login and signup page
description:
Reduce the auth page density and copy. Keep the needed functionality, but make the screen feel lighter and more direct.

## Todo
- [x] Remove redundant explanatory copy from the login/signup page
- [x] Simplify the auth layout so the primary action is more obvious
- [x] Verify the page feels cleaner without losing necessary guidance

## Summary of Changes

- removed most of the redundant auth-page copy and collapsed the layout into one primary card
- kept magic link as the obvious primary action while retaining the dev password fallback when enabled, with password sign-in using the same red primary treatment
- tightened the page overall, then added a little extra spacing above the auth buttons to keep the form rhythm consistent

title: Split active session into dedicated route
description:
Move the active session workflow off the /sessions overview page and into its own route so the sessions home can focus on starting and browsing history.

## Todo
- [ ] Add a dedicated active-session page route
- [ ] Redirect /sessions to the active route when a session is in progress
- [ ] Remove the empty no-active-session card from /sessions

title: Implement beautification visual system
description:
Apply a web-specific visual system inspired by the practice-pal mobile app palette and interaction language without copying its screen layouts.

## Todo
- [x] Update global design tokens, fonts, and background treatment for the web app
- [x] Restyle the app shell and navigation for the new palette and responsive framing
- [x] Apply shared surface, chip, and accent patterns across the main dashboard pages
- [x] Verify lint, typecheck, and build
- [x] Summarize the beautification pass and close the task

## Summary of Changes

Applied a calmer web visual system using the accepted reference palette rather than copying the mobile app styling. Updated the global theme, shell, navigation, and main dashboard surfaces to use the red/slate/orange color language with flatter backgrounds, quieter cards, and more restrained accents, then verified npm run lint, npm run typecheck, and npm run build.

title: Add drag-and-drop ordering for rows and lists
description:
After position fields are removed from the UI, replace manual ordering with direct manipulation. Add drag-and-drop ordering for rows and lists where order matters, including removing visible slot/order fields from session item editing and hidden section ordering metadata from the library list once drag-and-drop exists.

## Todo
- [x] Define drag-and-drop targets for sessions, setlists, and library ordering flows
- [x] Implement persistence for reordered rows
- [x] Verify drag-and-drop works reliably on desktop and acceptable on touch devices

## Summary of Changes

Implemented `dnd-kit` vertical sortable lists for book sections, setlist items, and active session items, backed by server-side reorder actions that persist the updated order. The earlier raw position UI has been replaced by drag handles and direct manipulation while preserving existing links and inline controls. Browser verification confirmed the interaction works and feels good.

title: Redesign library flow away from all-in-one page
description:
The current Library screen is too dense because it exposes every hierarchy and edit form on one page. Redesign it into clearer web-native flows with lighter summaries and focused edit/create surfaces.

## Todo
- [x] Define a clearer library information architecture for web
- [x] Implement a lighter Library overview instead of all edit forms inline
- [x] Move create and edit actions into focused flows or panels
- [x] Verify lint, typecheck, and build
- [x] Summarize the redesign pass and close the task

## Summary of Changes

Redesigned the library flow away from the all-in-one editor. Library is now a lighter overview with modal top-level creation, focused book and artist detail pages, and dedicated section builder routes.

title: Anonymous try-before-signup flow
description:
Allow anonymous users to try the website without creating a full account first.

## Todo
- [ ] Define what anonymous users can access and how long the session persists
- [ ] Implement the anonymous-user auth/data flow
- [ ] Verify users can try core workflows before signing up

title: Update AGENTS.md workflow guidance
description:
Update AGENTS.md with commit and PR expectations and a lightweight repository tree summary.

## Todo
- [ ] Review the current AGENTS.md content
- [ ] Add commit and PR expectations for changes
- [ ] Add a lightweight file tree overview
- [x] Verify the updated instructions read cleanly

## Summary of Changes

Expanded AGENTS.md beyond the beans-prime reminder to include commit and PR expectations and a lightweight repository tree overview.

title: Inline book header editing
description:
Simplify `/library/books/[bookId]` by removing the redundant top edit card. The title and author/composer should be edited directly in the page header area instead of splitting them across separate surfaces.

## Todo
- [x] Move book title editing into the top header area
- [x] Make author/composer editable alongside the title instead of showing it as a badge
- [x] Verify the book detail header feels lighter and clearer

## Summary of Changes

- moved book title and composer editing into the hero area instead of a separate top card
- kept the lighter header layout but restored a read-first default state with an Edit toggle
- simplified the book detail surface without forcing the page to live in edit mode

title: Allow editing preloaded setlist session items
description:
Sessions started from a setlist currently preload items but treat them like static rows. They should be editable like manually logged items.

## Todo
- [x] Add an explicit update path for existing session items
- [x] Expose inline editing for tempo and order on preloaded session rows
- [x] Verify editing works for setlist-started sessions in the browser

## Summary of Changes

Added inline editing for preloaded session rows so tempo and slot can be adjusted after starting from a setlist, matching normal logged-item behavior.

title: Modernize form controls and card hierarchy
description:
Refresh the shared form system so controls feel more current and readable.

## Todo
- [x] Replace the stale native select treatment with a more modern down-opening control
- [x] Increase the default type scale for form labels, inputs, and supporting UI text
- [x] Simplify card and section titles so hierarchy is clearer and accents are less noisy
- [x] Unify inconsistent button treatments and streamline session start controls
- [x] Verify the refreshed forms in the browser, then run lint/build and final typecheck

## Summary of Changes

Completed the current UI punch-up and shared-component-base pass. The app now leans more heavily on a unified theme and shared component primitives, the form system is more cohesive, titles and card hierarchy are cleaner, and the branch builds cleanly after removing the dev-only route-type import and remote font dependency. More focused follow-on polish remains in Beautification V2.

title: Collapse standalone stats surface into library
description:
Retire /stats as a primary workflow and route users to progress where they already manage library items.

## Todo
- [ ] Remove Stats from the primary side navigation
- [ ] Redirect or otherwise collapse /stats into the library flow
- [ ] Keep progress discoverable from the relevant library pages

title: UI V3
description:
Third-pass UI polish focused on navigation chrome, page copy reduction, modal/form spacing consistency, and remaining workflow-view improvements.

## Success Criteria
- navigation chrome feels more intentional and consistent
- spacing and copy are tighter across modals and pages
- session history and state/detail flows are easier to navigate

title: Fix session item save conflict handling
description:
Saving a session item from the frontend fails with PostgreSQL error 42P10 because the app uses ON CONFLICT on nullable columns, while the database uniqueness is enforced with an expression index.

## Todo
- [x] Replace the session item upsert with conflict-safe insert/update logic
- [x] Verify lint, typecheck, and build
- [x] Summarize the fix and close the bug bean

## Summary of Changes

Replaced the practice session item upsert with explicit lookup-and-update or insert logic so session item saves no longer rely on ON CONFLICT against nullable columns. Verified the change with npm run lint and npm run typecheck.

title: Vercel deployment and merge-ready delivery flow
description:
Make the app deployable from pull requests and merges without manual patchwork.

## Scope
- Vercel project wiring and environment strategy
- Build, lint, and typecheck expectations for CI
- Preview deployment support for PRs
- Production deployment path for main branch merges

## Summary of Changes

Completed the repository-side deployment path by adding CI verification and documenting the Vercel preview and production setup.


Deployment was verified through a working Vercel preview for PR #1.

title: Initial Structure
description:
Establish the base Next.js application, core host pages, and deployment pipeline so the app can ship from PRs and merges.

## Success Criteria
- App boots in all environments
- Main host areas exist in the shell
- Preview and production deployment path is working
- Base tooling is in place for subsequent feature work

## Summary of Changes

Delivered the Initial Structure milestone with a working Next.js app shell, core host routes, Supabase-ready plumbing, CI checks, and Vercel setup documentation.


This milestone was validated through a successful Vercel preview deployment before closure.

title: Library management routes and nested item selectors
description:
Build the library UI around the two supported hierarchy types from the spec.

## Scope
- CRUD pages and forms for books, sections, exercises, artists, and songs
- Goal tempo inputs and inherited defaults
- Nested search or picker UI for selecting practice items
- Library browsing aligned to the user's physical collection model

## Summary of Changes

Delivered the library UI around both supported hierarchy types with real CRUD forms, goal tempo/default presentation, and a reusable nested practice item selector. The resulting structures now feed the setlist and session flows.

title: Core visual system and responsive layout polish
description:
Refine the overall look and feel without changing the app's MVP scope.

## Scope
- Design tokens for spacing, typography, and surfaces
- Responsive layout tuning for phone-first use
- Cohesive navigation and page framing
- Lightweight styling aligned with the spec's clean aesthetic

## Summary of Changes

Completed the core visual system pass for the web app by translating the practice-pal reference into a calmer responsive shell. Established the accepted palette and page framing across the global theme, navigation, and major dashboard surfaces without copying the mobile layouts.

title: Bootstrap Next.js app and core toolchain
description:
Set up the base Next.js project from the empty repo.

## Scope
- Create package manifest and app directory structure
- Add TypeScript, Next.js, React, and baseline scripts
- Add lint, format, and local development commands
- Establish starter project files for the app shell

## Summary of Changes

Bootstrapped the Next.js App Router project, added TypeScript and core npm scripts, and established the baseline file structure for the app shell.

title: Beautification
description:
Polish the MVP UI with better CSS, stronger consistency, and a cleaner practice-first experience across devices.

## Success Criteria
- Visual system is cohesive and lightweight
- Mobile-first layouts feel intentional
- Empty, loading, and active states are polished
- The app is cleaner and more usable without changing core scope

## Summary of Changes

Completed the first beautification milestone with a cohesive visual system, lighter workflow screens, simplified library and setlist flows, cleaner sessions UX, reduced copy noise, and drag-and-drop ordering. Remaining follow-on polish and UX work were moved into separate new milestones so this shipped checkpoint can be closed cleanly.

title: Empty, loading, and active-session state polish
description:
Polish the states users will frequently encounter while practicing.

## Scope
- Empty-state content for new accounts and sparse data
- Loading and optimistic UI behavior
- Stronger emphasis for active session controls and interruption handling
- Finishing details for transitions, feedback, and state clarity

title: Configure Tailwind, DaisyUI, and shared theme primitives
description:
Add the styling stack and project-level design primitives for the MVP shell.

## Scope
- Tailwind and DaisyUI setup
- Global CSS and theme tokens
- Shared UI utilities and base styles
- Mobile-first defaults for subsequent pages

## Summary of Changes

Configured Tailwind CSS v4 and DaisyUI, added the project theme, and introduced the global styling primitives for the MVP shell.

title: Implement Initial Structure milestone
description:
Execute the Initial Structure milestone from an empty repo by scaffolding the app, defining the host pages, and setting up merge and PR deployment flow.

## Todo
- [x] Break the milestone epics into executable child beans
- [x] Scaffold the Next.js, DaisyUI, and Supabase-ready foundation
- [x] Implement the app shell, navigation, and host pages
- [x] Add merge and PR deployment workflow support
- [x] Verify the project builds cleanly
- [x] Summarize the implementation and any open decisions

## Summary of Changes

Executed the Initial Structure milestone from an empty repository. Created feature-level beans for each child epic, scaffolded the Next.js application with Tailwind, DaisyUI, and Supabase-ready helpers, built the responsive host shell and top-level routes, added CI checks, documented the Vercel deployment flow, and verified lint, typecheck, and production build success.

title: Translate SPEC.md into milestone and epic beans
description:
Turn the product spec into a concrete beans roadmap with milestone and epic hierarchy.

## Todo
- [x] Read and extract the main implementation tracks from SPEC.md
- [x] Create milestone beans for the core delivery phases
- [x] Create child epic beans with concrete scope under each milestone
- [x] Review the hierarchy for coverage against the spec
- [x] Add a summary of the resulting roadmap

## Summary of Changes

Created four milestone beans from the product spec: Initial Structure, Backend MVP, Frontend Definition, and Beautification. Added fourteen child epic beans covering stack setup, deployment, auth and ownership, schema design, session logging, stats queries, frontend route and form definition, setlist and library flows, and UI polish. Verified the milestone-to-epic hierarchy after creation.

title: Domain schema for library, sessions, and setlists
description:
Model the MVP entities described in the spec.

## Scope
- Books, sections, exercises, artists, and songs
- Setlists and setlist items
- Practice sessions and practiced-item joins
- Goal tempo storage and inheritance rules at the item level


Validated by applying the Backend MVP SQL migration successfully in Supabase.

title: Session routes and active timer workflow
description:
Define the session-related screens and user interactions.

## Scope
- Sessions index and session detail routes
- Active session screen with timer controls
- Start session flows from scratch or from a setlist
- Validation and editing surfaces for notes and tempo entries

## Summary of Changes

Implemented the sessions route with active-session controls, pause and resume behavior, note editing, session-item logging, recent session history, and start-from-scratch or start-from-setlist flows. Browser verification confirmed the session workflow works end to end.

title: Add Supabase-ready environment and client plumbing
description:
Prepare the project for Supabase without implementing the full backend MVP yet.

## Scope
- Environment variable contract
- Browser and server client helpers
- Shared backend integration boundaries
- Placeholder auth-aware app wiring for future milestones

## Summary of Changes

Added the Supabase environment contract and browser and server helper boundaries so future auth and data milestones can plug in cleanly.

title: User auth and row-level ownership
description:
Ensure every core record belongs to a user and is safely isolated.

## Scope
- Supabase auth integration
- User identity propagation through app and backend
- Row-level security or equivalent ownership controls
- User-scoped data access patterns


Validated with a live Supabase project using email magic links and working sign-in and sign-out flows.

title: Stats pages and chart controls
description:
Define the frontend for the MVP stats experiences.

## Scope
- Progress views for a single song or exercise
- Book or section completion views
- Time-range controls for common windows
- Core chart and summary components for trend display

## Summary of Changes

Implemented the stats route with item-progress and book-completion controls, common time-range filters, and summary components derived from live session data. Browser verification confirmed the stats views render correctly against the tested data.

title: Define route map and application shell
description:
Create the main shell and route structure for Sessions, Library, Setlists, and Stats.

## Scope
- Top-level navigation pattern
- Shared layout shell
- Route folders for each host area
- Default landing behavior for the app

## Summary of Changes

Defined the top-level route map and responsive application shell with desktop side navigation and mobile bottom navigation.

title: App shell, navigation, and host pages
description:
Establish the main product shell that exposes the core areas from the spec.

## Scope
- Primary navigation for Sessions, Library, Setlists, and Stats
- Base route structure and placeholder host pages
- Mobile-first shell and layout primitives
- Entry points for active session visibility

## Summary of Changes

Completed the responsive application shell and deployed host pages for Sessions, Library, Setlists, and Stats.

title: Form, list, and data-display refinement
description:
Improve the readability and usability of dense workflow screens.

## Scope
- Better form hierarchy and inline validation styling
- More legible list, table, and card treatments
- Clear emphasis for goals, tempo history, and progress summaries
- Consistent iconography and interaction affordances

## Summary of Changes

Completed the accepted form, list, and data-display refinement work for the first beautification milestone, including the library and setlists redesigns, session-flow cleanup, copy reduction, DaisyUI standardization, flatter card hierarchy, click-target improvements, visible position cleanup, and `dnd-kit`-based reordering. Remaining polish work was moved into Beautification V2.

title: Frontend Definition
description:
Define and implement the product pages, routes, forms, and key UI elements needed to exercise the MVP end to end.

## Success Criteria
- Primary routes and navigation are defined
- Key create/edit/logging flows exist
- Core forms validate the expected inputs
- The frontend covers sessions, library, setlists, and stats

## Summary of Changes

Implemented the MVP frontend across library, setlists, sessions, and stats with real data-driven routes, forms, and key UI elements. Local browser verification confirmed the end-to-end UI flow works, including auth fallback for development, library management, setlists, session logging, and stats rendering.

title: Add CI checks for pull requests and merge safety
description:
Ensure pull requests and merges exercise the core quality gates.

## Scope
- GitHub Actions or equivalent CI config
- Lint, typecheck, and build verification
- Fast feedback for pull requests
- Main branch protection-ready checks

## Summary of Changes

Added a GitHub Actions workflow that runs npm ci, lint, typecheck, and build on pull requests and on pushes to main.

title: Document and configure Vercel deployment flow
description:
Prepare the repository for preview and production deployments on Vercel.

## Scope
- Vercel configuration files if needed
- Environment variable documentation
- Preview versus production deployment expectations
- Repo setup notes for GitHub to Vercel integration

## Summary of Changes

Documented the Vercel setup and environment expectations and prepared the repository for preview and production deployments from GitHub.

title: Stats queries for tempo progress and book completion
description:
Back the MVP analytics described in the spec with reusable query logic.

## Scope
- Global max tempo history per song or exercise
- Progress-to-goal calculations over time
- Book or section completion calculations over time
- Time-windowed stats for week, month, 6 month, year, and all time


Stats query helpers are now backed by the live Backend MVP schema and ready for frontend integration.

title: Setlist creation and session-start flows
description:
Define the setlist UX so sessions can be planned and launched quickly.

## Scope
- Setlist list, create, edit, and manage routes
- Add and remove mixed library items in a setlist
- Start a practice session from a setlist
- Allow adding more items after a setlist-backed session starts

## Summary of Changes

Implemented the setlists route with create and edit flows, mixed-item add and remove behavior, and integration with session start. Verified locally that setlist-backed sessions and later item additions both work.

title: Scaffold Next.js, DaisyUI, and Supabase foundation
description:
Create the baseline application stack and shared project wiring.

## Scope
- Next.js app scaffold and baseline project structure
- DaisyUI and Tailwind setup
- Supabase client/server integration points
- Shared environment and config conventions

## Summary of Changes

Completed the initial application foundation with Next.js, TypeScript, Tailwind, DaisyUI, and Supabase-ready helper boundaries.

title: Build host pages and placeholder states
description:
Implement the initial pages so each core area exists and is deployable.

## Scope
- Sessions, Library, Setlists, and Stats host pages
- Placeholder copy and empty states
- Shared cards, headers, and section framing
- Active-session entry point placeholder

## Summary of Changes

Built the Sessions, Library, Setlists, and Stats host pages with placeholder states and reserved entry points for future workflows.

title: Session logging, tempo capture, and post-session edits
description:
Support the primary write flows for practice tracking.

## Scope
- Start, pause, resume, and end session lifecycle
- Associate one or more library items to a session
- Record tempo values and session notes
- Edit completed session tempo entries and notes


Backend session paths are now wired into the live Supabase schema and ready for frontend integration.

title: Backend MVP
description:
Implement the core backend model from the spec, including all primary tables and user-scoped control over data.

## Success Criteria
- Core domain tables exist for sessions, library, setlists, and stats inputs
- Data is user-owned and protected
- Core write and read paths support the MVP product flows
- Post-session editing and stats queries are backed by real data

## Summary of Changes

Backend MVP is now complete in code and validated against a live Supabase project. The project has a working magic-link auth flow, the full MVP schema and RLS layer, backend services for core entities and sessions, and stats query support ready for frontend wiring.
